topic: Memphis BBQ — dry rub vs wet debate

turns.content: man settle something for me. dry rub or wet sauce on ribs
Ha, that's a loaded question depending on where you're from. I'd say both have their place, but what's your take? You sound like you've got strong feelings on this one.
dry rub all day long. if you need to drown your ribs in sauce thats just covering up bad meat

That's a fair point. A good dry rub lets the quality of the meat speak for itself. The smoke and the spice do the heavy lifting. Sauce on the side for dipping is one thing, but slathering it on before you even taste the rib? That's a different conversation.
exactly. i dont mind sauce on the table but dont bring me ribs already soaked in it. thats not bbq thats a mess

Agreed. There's something about biting into a rib where you can taste the bark, the smoke ring, the seasoning all working together. You got a favorite spot you go to, or are you more of a backyard smoker?
central bbq is hard to beat around here. but i do my own ribs on the weekends sometimes when the weather cooperates. got a weber smokey mountain

The Weber Smokey Mountain is a solid choice. Holds temperature well and doesn't need constant babysitting. Central BBQ has a great reputation too — their dry rub ribs are always near the top of people's lists. How long do you usually run yours on the smoker?
about 5 hours give or take. low and slow around 225. i use a pretty simple rub. paprika, garlic, brown sugar, little cayenne. nothing fancy

That's the way to do it. Simple rub, low and slow, let time and smoke do the work. Some people overthink it with fifteen different spices and end up with a muddy flavor. Sounds like you've got your method dialed in. You wrap them at any point or go unwrapped the whole time?
i wrap in butcher paper around the 3 hour mark. keeps them moist but still gets a good bark. foil makes them too steamy for my taste

Butcher paper is the move. You still get airflow so the bark stays intact, but it pushes through the stall faster. Foil basically braises them and you lose that texture on the outside. Sounds like you know what you're doing out there.
appreciate it man. alright now im hungry and its 9 in the morning. bad timing on my part
Ha, nothing wrong with thinking about ribs before lunch. Maybe that's your sign to fire up the smoker this weekend. Enjoy it when you do.
